title: Infinite URL Spaces, Calendars, Facets, Sessions, and Budget Defense · Type-System Trace: Trap Stop Evidence

Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to contain calendar, facet, session, template, soft-error, and query-cardinality traps with stop evidence; release evidence binds source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, lockfile, package, browser revision, container, configuration, and restored state; then change one test, mutation, package, browser binary, container, capacity, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Stop the smallest affected scope, retain trigger and frontier counts, notify owner, and require explicit restart policy.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Trap Stop Evidence model · Theory Trap Stop Evid…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Trap handling should silently skip suspicious URLs and continue.
